title: multigraph action-name rule + ≤1 unlabeled edge
manager: The rules that make FSL a proper multigraph: multiple edges can connect the same two states as long as their action names disambiguate them, and at most one unlabeled edge is allowed between a pair, because more would be genuinely ambiguous. This is what lets a machine have several distinct transitions between the same two states without the graph becoming undecidable about which one a given action meant.
cs: multiple edges per state-pair keyed by action name; at most one unlabeled edge per ordered pair; further ambiguity is a compile error; underpins the resolution order (W6.89).
